zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Elizabeth York於1693年4月10日出生在英國巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼時，她的父親Richard York II42歲，母親Ann Seymour41歲。她於1716年9月29日與John Glover在英國巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1779年在美國北卡羅來納州蘭多夫享年86歲，埋葬在美國北卡羅來納州蘭多夫格雷斯 查阿培爾奧德爾 特洛格頓 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Elizabeth York于1693年4月10日出生在英国巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼时，她的父亲Richard York II42岁，母亲Ann Seymour41岁。她于1716年9月29日与John Glover在英国巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1779年在美国北卡罗来纳州蘭多夫享年86岁，埋葬在美国北卡罗来纳州蘭多夫格雷斯 查阿培爾奧德爾 特洛格頓 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: York
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of York in northern England. The surname is now widespread throughout England. Originally, the city bore the Latin name Eburacum, which is probably from a Brittonic name meaning ‘yew-tree place’. This was altered by folk etymology to Old English Eoforwīc (from the elements eofor ‘wild boar’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’). This name was taken over by Scandinavian settlers, who altered it back to opacity in the form Jórvík or Jórk (English York, which became finally settled as the placename in the 13th century). The surname has also been adopted by Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of the East Slavic patronymic   Yurkovich   or its Croatian, Slovak, or Slovenian variants. Compare   Yurk  .

variants.name: Yurkovich
meanings: Ukrainian (standard transliteration Yurkovych) and Belorussian: patronymic from the personal name Yurko and its Belorussian equivalent Yurka, pet forms of Ukrainian Yuriy and Belorussian Yuray, equivalents of Greek Geōrgios (see George ).
Americanized form of Croatian Jurković, Slovak and Slovenian Jurkovič (see Jurkovic , compare Jurkovich and Yurkovic ).
Jewish (from Bessarabia): habitational name for someone from Yurkovtsy in Ukraine.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

historicEvents.title: Gregorian Calendar is Adopted
historicEvents.description: Gregorian calendar was adopted in England in 1752. That year, Wednesday, September 2, 1752, was followed by Thursday, September 14th, 1752, which caused the country to skip ahead eleven days.

historicEvents.title: Seven Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Seven Years' War began as a North American conflict then stretched between England and France. England, along with allies, battled France in America, India, and Europe, making it arguably the first global war. The conflict ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1763 and England was victorious. The Seven Years' war ultimately led to discontent in the colonies and the American Revolution.

historicEvents.title: Boston Tea Party
historicEvents.description: Thousands of British troops were sent to Boston to enforce Britain's tax laws. Taxes were repealed on all imports to the American Colonies except tea. Americans, disguised as Native Americans, dumped chests of tea imported by the East India Company into the Boston Harbor in protest. This escalated tensions between the American Colonies and the British government.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elizabeth York was born on 10 April 1693, in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Richard York II, was 42 and her mother, Ann Seymour, was 41. She married John Glover on 29 September 1716, in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1779, in Randolph, North Carolina, United States, at the age of 86, and was buried in Odell Trogdon Cemetery, Grays Chapel, Randolph, North Carolina, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Elizabeth York est née le 10 avril 1693, à Olney, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard York II, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Ann Seymour, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé John Glover le 29 septembre 1716, à Olney, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1779, à Randolph, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis, à l’âge de 86 ans, et a été enterrée à Odell Trogdon Cemetery, Grays Chapel, Randolph, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis.

September 1716 in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England John Glover geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth York è nata il il 10 aprile 1693 nel Olney, Buckinghamshire, England, sua padre, Richard York II, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Ann Seymour, aveva 41 anni. Lei sposato John Glover il 29 settembre 1716, nel Olney, Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1779, nel Randolph, Carolina del Nord, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 86 anni, e fu sepolta a Odell Trogdon Cemetery, Grays Chapel, Randolph, Carolina del Nord, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Yorkが1693年4月10日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアオルニーで生まれた時，父Richard York IIは42歳，母Ann Seymourは41歳であった。1716年9月29日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアオルニーでJohn Gloverと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1779年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ランドルフで86歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ランドルフグレイズ チャペルオデル トログドン セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth York(이)가 1693년 4월 10일 영국 벅잉함실르 니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard York II(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Seymour(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1716년 9월 29일 영국 벅잉함실르 니에서 John Glover(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1779년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프 그라이스 차플 오데 트로그던 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth York nasceu em 10 de abril de 1693, em Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard York II, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Ann Seymour, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com John Glover em 29 de setembro de 1716, em Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1779, em Randolph, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos da América, com 86 anos, e foi sepultada em Odell Trogdon Cemetery, Grays Chapel, Randolph, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Wicomico, Northumberland, Virginia, Brit: викомику, нортумбирланд, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Elizabeth York родилась 10 апреля 1693 г. в местности олни, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard York II было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Ann Seymour, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Glover 29 сентября 1716 г. в местности олни, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1779 г. в местности рандолф, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 86 лет и была похоронена в местности оделл трогдон кеметири, греис чапел, рандолф, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Elizabeth York nació el 10 de abril de 1693, en Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard York II, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Ann Seymour, tenía 41 años. Se casó con John Glover el 29 de septiembre de 1716, en Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1779, en Randolph, Carolina del Norte, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultada en Odell Trogdon Cemetery, Grays Chapel, Randolph, Carolina del Norte, Estados Unidos.
